summary: Adjuvant aromatase inhibitors reduce systemic estrogen exposure rather than increasing it, avoiding the well-documented cardiovascular risks associated with exogenous hormone supplementation. Large trial data show no significant rise in heart attacks, strokes, or other cardiovascular events linked to these drugs compared to placebo or tamoxifen in postmenopausal women with early-stage breast cancer.

Large trial data show no significant rise in heart attacks, strokes, or other cardiovascular events linked to these drugs compared to placebo or tamoxifen in postmenopausal women with early-stage breast cancer.. Answer choice: B
